findings.title: Single-question answers are dropped after the pending entry is removed twice
findings.description: The button/select/modal handlers now remove `pending_permissions[request_id]` before calling `handle_question_answer`, but the single-question branch inside `handle_question_answer` removes the same entry again before sending the oneshot. For non-group questions that second lookup returns `None`, so `PermissionDecision::Answer(...)` is never sent and the subprocess stays stuck waiting for input.
findings.suggestion: Use the already-removed `PendingPermission` to send the answer directly, or change `handle_question_answer` to accept the pending entry/response sender instead of re-reading `pending_permissions` for the single-question path.

findings.title: Multi-question groups are registered only after all messages are sent, creating a lost-answer race
findings.description: In the multi-question branch, each sub-question `PendingPermission` is inserted as soon as its message is sent, but the corresponding `PendingQuestionGroup` is inserted only after the entire send loop completes. A fast user can answer the first message during that window: the interaction path removes the sub-question pending entry, `handle_question_answer` looks up the not-yet-inserted group, and the answer is silently discarded. That leaves the worker waiting forever for `group.total` answers.
findings.suggestion: Insert the `PendingQuestionGroup` before sending any sub-question messages, or buffer/rollback interactions until the group exists. At minimum, treat a missing group as an error and deny/clean up instead of dropping the answer silently.

findings.title: Truncating the full sub-request ID can erase the question suffix and break Discord component routing
findings.description: `create_question_message_for_index` builds component IDs from `truncate_request_id(sub_request_id)`, where `sub_request_id` already contains `__q{idx}`. If the base request ID is near or above 80 characters, truncation can remove that suffix so multiple questions produce the same `custom_id`, or produce an ID that no longer matches the key stored in `pending_permissions`. That violates the Discord `custom_id` constraint handling you were trying to satisfy and can misroute or drop interactions.
findings.suggestion: Encode the question index outside the truncated portion, or hash/shorten the base request ID first so the stored lookup key and emitted `custom_id` stay stable and unique within Discord's 100-character limit.

summary: The multi-question design is close, but the current diff introduces one blocking regression in single-question answer delivery and two state/routing bugs around group registration and custom_id truncation.
